files_deferred: Live vendor adapters (Tradier/Tastytrade/Massive/Yahoo) that perform network I/O -> they implement the MarketDataProvider protocol added here; the deterministic StaticProvider is the offline substrate. Network adapters land when credentials/integration tests are available.
gzip on-disk recording format (System A uses .jsonl.gz) -> plain JSONL for now; deterministic and reviewable. gzip is an additive wrapper later.
behavior_preserved: Ordered failover: first provider to produce a tick wins; the winner and fallback state are recorded (composite_feed.CompositeFeed).
Settlement has a dedicated backstop provider, separate from the chain feed failover (Yahoo settlement role).
Per-session integer sequence on every recorded tick (chain_store seq); replay is deterministic and requires no network.
intentional_changes: Providers emit a typed RawTick and the composite assembles canonical snapshots via the Phase 1 assembler, rather than passing provider TickSnapshot objects downstream (spec §13.1).
Recording is self-describing with a per-record content hash; replay fails closed on hash mismatch, sequence gap, schema mismatch, and malformed records (spec §15).
Recording serialized as deterministic canonical JSONL (identical snapshots -> identical bytes).
known_gaps: Live vendor network adapters are deferred (see files_deferred); the protocol and offline substrate are in place.
Recorded-tick parity against a RUNNING System A recording remains future work; the fixture here locks System B's own deterministic record/replay contract.
Replay yields canonical snapshot dicts; rehydration into frozen CanonicalMarketSnapshot instances is added when a downstream consumer needs typed replay (later phase).
rollback: Revert the Phase 2 commit. The Phase 1 ingestion foundation is unaffected; no runtime services or persisted state are introduced.
No new third-party dependencies were added in Phase 2.
next_phase: Phase 3 — RND and structural features (spec §63): migrate rnd_extractor.py, GEX variants (gex/ package), flip/walls, volatility, structural state, persistent adaptive state, and parity tests.
